title: Game State, Scenes, Pause, Life Loss, Restart, and Ownership · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent boot, menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, settings, and exit as explicit valid states.
Define trigger, guard, exit work, entry work, resulting state, and rejected transitions.
Freeze simulation clocks and dangerous input while keeping menus, status, audio control, and window events responsive.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug State Model
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 9298 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent boot, menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, settings, and exit as explicit valid states.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Scene Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-scene-evidence-uet7hi" that uses case 3069 to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Scene Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Scene Evidence, keep Scene Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-scene-evidence-uet7hi
# Competency: asteroids-scene-evidence.
# Case 3069: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_scene_evidence_uet7hi(states: int = 6, valid_transitions: int = 9, invalid_transitions: int = 0, pause_freezes_simulation: bool = True, restart_resets_run: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_uet7hi = "uet7hi-ih7teu-3069"
    assert evidence_variant_uet7hi
    evidence_axes_uet7hi = (
        "playeruet7hi",
        "seed3069",
        "inputih7teu",
        "frameuet7hi3069",
        "state3069uet7hi",
        "playtestih7teu3069",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_uet7hi)) == 6
    if states < 4 or valid_transitions < states:
        return False, states, "state-graph"
    if invalid_transitions > 0:
        return False, invalid_transitions, "illegal-transition"
    if not pause_freezes_simulation:
        return False, valid_transitions, "pause-leak"
    if not restart_resets_run:
        return False, valid_transitions, "restart-leak"
    assert valid_transitions >= states
    return True, valid_transitions, "explicit-scenes"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Scene Evidence structural check is only one gate. explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
